knownObservedWindow.note: Observed VM-20 window includes reserve mechanics plus exclusion-test transition language.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on VM-20 only.
Do not widen into other source families.
Keep VM-01, VM-30, VM-31, and VM-G references review-only unless the same batch intentionally includes the referenced section.
Keep stale, superseded, repealed, or companion guidance as notes only.
exclusions: No raw source files in Git.

proposedBatches.title: VM-20 framework overview

pageTarget.notes: Refine by heading and intro pages during preparation.

proposedBatches.rationale: Pin the chapter boundary and terminology before mechanics.

proposedBatches.title: Net premium reserve mechanics

pageTarget.notes: Pin after the overview batch isolates the NPR language.

proposedBatches.rationale: Isolate NPR formulas so they do not leak into the other reserve concepts.

proposedBatches.title: Deterministic reserve mechanics

pageTarget.notes: Pin after NPR because the deterministic reserve text should stay separate.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep deterministic reserve treatment isolated from NPR and SR content.

proposedBatches.title: Stochastic reserve mechanics

pageTarget.notes: Pin after DR because stochastic reserve modeling should stay separate.

proposedBatches.rationale: Separate stochastic reserve modeling from deterministic and net premium language.

proposedBatches.title: Exclusion tests

pageTarget.notes: Split eligibility language away from reserve mechanics.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep exclusion language separate because it often overlaps with boundary transition text.

proposedBatches.title: Assumptions and margins

pageTarget.notes: Capture only after the mechanics are isolated.

proposedBatches.rationale: Assumptions and margins need their own review lane because they often require actuarial judgment.

proposedBatches.title: Asset assumptions

pageTarget.notes: Keep asset assumptions separate from reserve formulas.

proposedBatches.rationale: Asset assumptions often cross-reference other sections and should be isolated.

proposedBatches.title: Reinsurance

pageTarget.notes: Treat ceded reserve text as its own review lane.

proposedBatches.rationale: Reinsurance treatment tends to cross-reference other guidance and should remain isolated.

proposedBatches.title: Documentation and reporting cross-references

pageTarget.notes: Capture cross-references without absorbing the full reporting instructions.

proposedBatches.rationale: Documentation references should stay separate from the operational instructions they point to.

proposedBatches.title: Definitions and cross-references handled elsewhere

pageTarget.notes: Use this as a cleanup batch for lookup-only material.

proposedBatches.rationale: Finish with definitions and lookup-only references so they do not clutter the mechanics batches.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat explicit must/shall/required language as the requirement; preserve the exact citation and the chapter context.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat introduction, examples, chapter navigation, and explanatory prose as background unless the text imposes an obligation.
reviewStandards.actuarialJudgment: Treat assumptions, margins, asset treatment, reserve interpretation, and reinsurance treatment as human actuarial judgment unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceHandling: Tag VM-01, VM-30, VM-31, and VM-G references as review-only unless the same batch also captures the referenced operational section.
reviewStandards.staleOrCompanionGuidance: Tag repealed, superseded, or companion guidance as review-only support and keep it out of learner-facing output.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
Review standards include cross-reference handling and stale guidance handling.
futureChecksSuggested: Section boundary checks for batches 003 through 012.
Cross-reference flag coverage for VM-01, VM-30, VM-31, and VM-G.
No learner-facing or app-ready promotion checks.
Batch-definition synchronization when real VM-20 batches are created.
scriptImplications: scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should eventually gain the planned VM-20 batches when extraction is authorized.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should continue to treat the plan as configuration-only until extraction begins.

notes: Planning artifact only. No VM-20 extraction run is authorized by this config.
